positionData.text: Golden Eagles, this campaign is about us.
It’s about every student balancing responsibilities, working toward their goals, and navigating challenges along the way.
I am running for ASI President because I believe our student government should be accessible, transparent, and truly representative of the students it serves.

Associated Students, Inc. is the official voice of the student body. As President, I will serve as Chair of the Board of Directors and represent students in conversations with university leadership. While this role does not make every decision on campus, it does have the power to advocate, amplify student concerns, and push for meaningful improvements. That is exactly what I intend to do.

My priority is simple: making sure students feel heard and informed. Too often, students feel disconnected from the decisions that impact their daily lives. I will work to improve communication between ASI and the student body, increase transparency, and create more opportunities for students to share their input and concerns.

I also want to focus on the everyday challenges students face. Issues like access to basic needs resources, mental health support, and navigating campus services continue to affect many in our CSULA community. While ASI cannot solve these issues alone, it can play a key role in advocating for students, collaborating with campus partners, and raising awareness about available resources.

In addition, I am committed to strengthening student engagement. Every student should feel like they have a place on this campus. I will work to support inclusive programming, encourage involvement in student organizations, and uplift voices that may not always be heard.
At the end of the day, leadership in ASI is about representation and service. It is about showing up, listening, and advocating for students in spaces where their voices matter.
I am running for ASI President because I care about this campus and the people in it. I am ready to represent you, to listen to you, and to work alongside you.
I kindly ask for your vote!
Hi Golden Eagles! My name is Natalie Torres, and I'm running for Vice President for External Affairs. My full platform will be available soon.

Hello, my name is Jesus Corona, and I am running for Vice President for Community Engagement. I am the best candidate for this position because I do not see community as just a word. I see it as a responsibility. I want every student at Cal State LA to feel welcomed, included, and aware of the resources, events, and opportunities available to them.

My involvement on campus has prepared me to serve in this role with energy and purpose. I am currently active in organizations including the Pre-Law Society, the National Society of Leadership and Success, and the Philosophy Club, where I have continued building leadership, communication, and teamwork skills. Through these experiences, I have learned how important it is to create spaces where students feel seen, supported, and encouraged to participate.

What makes me the strongest candidate is my commitment to action. I want to strengthen the connection between students and campus life by promoting engagement, highlighting student voices, and helping create events and initiatives that bring people together. I believe leadership begins with service, and service begins with showing up for others.
If elected, I will work to make community engagement more visible, more inclusive, and more meaningful for all students.
Hey LA!
My name is Bryan Alvarado Chavez, and I'm running to be your next Vice President of Finance!

During my time at Cal State LA, I've not only been a student leader but also an active member of our campus community. Ever since I made the crazy decision to move out of my hometown, I have not looked back. Living in LA has made me a more budget-conscious geek than I ever was. I've had to budget my savings to afford the materials I need for class, whether it's online accounting tools or simple textbooks, just like you. Through these challenges, I have gained a deeper understanding of the challenges many of us face as students.

My experience with the Hispanic Business Society has been nothing short of amazing. As a Board Directer I’ve had the incredible opportunity to give back to this campus. In the past few semesters, I’ve welcomed new members with open arms. Had countless meaningful conversations with students, helping them find the most affordable food options on campus, or helping them find resources they need to succeed.

As a first-generation Latino student moving from Salinas to LA, I have experienced firsthand the support and resources available, some of which are funded by our student fees. My vision is to expand those resources across our entire campus. If elected, some of my goals include maximizing the value of student fees, ensuring students have access to the resources they need, and increasing financial transparency.
Thank you, and We Are LA!
Hi Golden Eagles! My name is Ekiria Magala, and I'm running for College of Ethnic Studies Representative. My full platform will be available soon.

data.sectionName: College of Arts and Letters Representative

positionData.text: I am running for College of Arts & Letters Representative to ensure students are heard, represented, and connected. Cal State LA sits in the heart of East LA—a place shaped by culture, resilience, and generations of people who fought to have their voices heard. That legacy inspires my leadership.

I am committed to being a bridge between students, faculty, and leadership—so that students in Arts & Letters and the broader student body feel seen, heard, and represented. I will work to strengthen communication, elevate student concerns, and help create meaningful change.
Instagram: cinnamonlacanela

data.sectionName: College of Health and Human Services Representative

positionData.text: Hey Golden Eagles! I'm Hermione Hsu, a third-year Public Health major from Pasadena, California. In my time outside of academics, I enjoy hiking, puzzles, and long conversations.

Throughout my college years, I've experienced firsthand the obstacles of navigating an unfamiliar environment and made mistakes that could've been prevented with the right guidance. As a candidate for College Representative of Health and Human Services, I am committed to serving as a strong student voice and bridging the gap between our student body and faculty through full transparency and accountability. If given the opportunity, I will advocate for clear communication so that students may feel fully supported and confident navigating their college paths.

data.sectionName: College of Natural and Social Sciences Representative

positionData.text: Hi Golden Eagles! My name is Maria Garcia Marroquin, and I'm running for College of Natural and Social Sciences Representative. My full platform will be available soon.

My name is Brandon Torres, and I am running for the Student Senator position. I would make a great candidate because as a current student, I feel like I can represent and support students. Also, as next year will be my last year, I want to be able to vote on changes that can help and benefit students in their academic journey. I want to improve the school and help students. I will be a committed student senator who attends meetings, stays organized, and follows through on their promises. I want to be here to listen to students’ concerns and clearly share those ideas with school leadership. Being able to speak up and express ideas is important. The only social media I plan to use for now is Instagram (btorres_223). Thank you.

Why I’m the Best Candidate: I’m running for Senator to represent my peers and support the advancement of this University. I aim to bring student voices into conversations and advocate for what improves student satisfaction. I’ve been a Golden Eagle for three years now, and I’ve seen how student input can make a difference. It just takes 1 person to speak up for others to join. At a commuter school like ours, it can be harder for students to stay involved or feel connected, which makes it even more important to create space for their expression. That’s why I felt the urge to take that step in. I will listen, reach out, and make sure students' perspectives are included in Academic Senate discussions. I believe I’m the best candidate for this role because I genuinely care about listening and representing others.

My name is Mark Raspopov. I am currently a junior at Cal State LA majoring in Computer Science and minoring in Biomedical Engineering, Psychology, and Math. I'm passionate about policy development and student success. I have been serving as an Undergraduate Academic Senator for the past two years, and I am running for the same position for next year. As a senator, I worked to address student concerns, improve academic policies, and promote initiatives that enhance our learning environment. Additionally, my involvement with the U-SU Board of Directors as a Student Director & Fiscal Committee Chair and with the AI/ML Club as President has prepared me to effectively advocate for your needs.

If you re-elect me, I'll strengthen communication between students and faculty and advocate for policies that enhance student success. I'm committed to making real change that improves our academic experience. Your voice matters, and I'll ensure it's heard. Let’s continue working together for a better future at Cal State LA!
Connect with me: https://www.linkedin.com/in/markraspopov
https://markraspopov.com
https://www.instagram.com/mark._._.123/
Thank you!

I am Julia Ryen Johnston, a candidate for Graduate Academic Senator. I believe I can serve as an approachable and trusted resource for sharing concerns, ideas, and experiences, and turn that feedback into thoughtful, actionable advocacy. I have been consistently involved in our community and heard my peers, I know we need a reliable bridge between students and faculty that will foster a more connected Cal State LA.

Through experience in research, leadership, and collaboration, I have developed the ability to navigate complex academic environments while maintaining my guiding principles. I value transparency, inclusivity, and clear communication, and I strive to ensure that every student feels represented. I am passionate about implementing lasting positive change and would bring a genuine commitment to uplifting our graduate student body.
LinkedIn | https://www.linkedin.com/in/juliaryenjohnston/
